heroPoems.title: If We Must Die
heroPoems.firstStanza:
If we must die—let it not be like hogs
Hunted and penned in an inglorious spot,
While round us bark the mad and hungry dogs,
Making their mock at our accursed lot.
If we must die—O let us nobly die,
So that our precious blood may not be shed

heroPoems.title: The wind gives me enough fallen leaves
heroPoems.firstStanza:
The wind gives me enough fallen leaves
to keep a small fire going.

heroPoems.title: Qualities in Rotation
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Clarity lifts.
Passion drives.
Inertia drags.

heroPoems.title: Sonnets to Orpheus I, 9
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Only he who raised the lyre
among shadows
may also guess
what endless praise is.

heroPoems.title: A Cooking Egg
heroPoems.firstStanza:
En l'an trentiesme de mon aage
Que toutes mes hontes j'ay beues...
heroPoems.tagline: For when the street goes quiet.

heroPoems.title: Still Waters
heroPoems.firstStanza:
He leads me
beside still waters.
He restores
my soul.
heroPoems.tagline: For when language keeps you company.

heroPoems.title: A sigh needs a lifetime to arrive at impact.
heroPoems.firstStanza:
A sigh needs a lifetime to arrive at impact.
Who rushes grief misunderstands physics.

heroPoems.title: The Recall
heroPoems.firstStanza:
I am the land of their fathers.
In me the virtue stays.
I will bring back my children,
After certain days.

heroPoems.title: Bright Star
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Bright star, would I were stedfast as thou art—
Not in lone splendour hung aloft the night
And watching, with eternal lids apart,
Like Nature's patient, sleepless Eremite,
The moving waters at their priestlike task
Of pure ablution round earth's human shores,
heroPoems.tagline: For when language keeps you company.

heroPoems.title: Within this earthen vessel are bowers and groves, and within it
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Within this earthen vessel are bowers and groves, and within it
is the Creator:
Within this vessel are the seven oceans and the unnumbered stars.
The touchstone and the jewel-appraiser are within;
And within this vessel the Eternal soundeth, and the spring wells
up.

collections.name: Where Stone Learns Water
collections.theme: A restrained current of longing that never announces itself directly.

featuredPoem.title: First Fig
featuredPoem.firstLines:
My candle burns at both ends;
It will not last the night;

poems.title: I found this cracked wooden bowl in bamboo

collections.name: The Distance Inside Home
collections.theme: Poems for the slow pivot from day-noise toward interior quiet.

featuredPoem.title: As Is Faith, So Is the Life
featuredPoem.firstLines:
You become
what you bow to

poems.title: An old grave at the foot of a quiet hill

collections.name: A Room Made of Waiting
collections.tagline: For when the night keeps watch.
collections.theme: A thread of attention to rain, glass, paper, and hands at rest.

featuredPoem.title: Chasing the Wind
featuredPoem.firstLines:
Smoke of smoke—everything is smoke.
What does a person gain

poems.title: The Way of Love and Steadiness
My life had stood—a loaded gun—
From heaven's garden a west wind crossed my field
Stopping by Woods on Snowy Evening
